Title: Successfully add a new user with valid data
Preconditions: 1. User Management page is accessible | 2. Database is running | 3. Server is running on port 5000
Steps: 1. Navigate to /users page | 2. Fill in Name field with valid name | 3. Fill in Date of Birth field with valid date (18+ years old) | 4. Fill in Address field with complete address (min 10 chars) | 5. Click "Add User" button | 6. Verify success message appears | 7. Verify user appears in table below form
Test Data: Name: "John Doe" | DOB: "1990-05-15" | Address: "123 Main Street, New York, NY 10001"
Expected Result: 1. Success message displays: "User added successfully!" | 2. Form fields are cleared | 3. New user appears in table with correct data | 4. Table shows: Name, DOB (formatted), Address, Created At timestamp | 5. User count in table increases by 1

Title: Complete user management workflow from navigation to data persistence
Preconditions: 1. Application is running | 2. Database is initialized | 3. User is on Landing Page
Steps: 1. Click "Users" link in navigation | 2. Verify navigation to /users page | 3. Fill user form with valid data | 4. Submit form | 5. Verify success message | 6. Refresh page | 7. Verify user persists in table | 8. Navigate away to /form page | 9. Return to /users page | 10. Verify user still exists in table
Test Data: Name: "Alice Smith" | DOB: "1985-12-20" | Address: "456 Oak Avenue, Los Angeles, CA 90001"
Expected Result: 1. Navigation works from all pages (/, /form, /kyc) | 2. User form submits successfully | 3. Data persists in database after page refresh | 4. Table displays all users consistently | 5. No data loss when navigating between pages | 6. Created timestamp is accurate

Title: Submit form with minimum valid data requirements
Preconditions: 1. User Management page is loaded | 2. Form is empty
Steps: 1. Enter name with exactly 2 characters | 2. Enter DOB for user exactly 18 years old today | 3. Enter address with exactly 10 characters | 4. Click "Add User" button
Test Data: Name: "Jo" | DOB: "<18 years ago from today>" | Address: "1234567890"
Expected Result: 1. Form accepts minimum valid data | 2. Validation passes | 3. User is created successfully | 4. Success message displays | 5. User appears in table with correct formatting

Title: Verify date formatting and timezone handling
Preconditions: 1. Users with various DOB exist in database | 2. User Management page is loaded
Steps: 1. Add user with DOB in different year ranges (1950s, 1980s, 2005) | 2. Verify date display formatting in table | 3. Check created_at timestamp formatting | 4. Verify dates display correctly regardless of user timezone
Expected Result: 1. All dates display in consistent format: "Month Day, Year" | 2. DOB shows as entered (no timezone shift) | 3. Created At shows full date and time | 4. Date formatting is locale-appropriate (en-US) | 5. No date calculation errors for edge years

Title: Handle empty user list on initial load
Preconditions: 1. Database is empty (no users exist) | 2. Navigate to /users page
Steps: 1. Load /users page | 2. Observe table section | 3. Verify empty state message | 4. Verify table structure is still present | 5. Add first user | 6. Verify table populates correctly
Test Data: No test data (empty database)
Expected Result: 1. Empty state displays: "No users found. Add your first user above!" | 2. No errors in console | 3. Form is still functional | 4. After adding first user, table displays correctly with headers and data | 5. Page transitions smoothly from empty to populated state

Title: Form validation provides immediate feedback
Preconditions: 1. User Management page is loaded | 2. Form fields are empty
Steps: 1. Click in Name field and type 1 character, then tab out | 2. Select a future date for DOB | 3. Enter 5 characters in Address field | 4. Observe validation messages | 5. Correct all fields to valid values | 6. Observe validation messages clear
Test Data: Invalid: Name="A", DOB="<future date>", Address="12345" | Valid: Name="John Smith", DOB="1992-08-15", Address="789 Elm St, City, State 12345"
Expected Result: 1. Inline error appears for Name: "Name must be at least 2 characters" | 2. Error for DOB: "Date of birth must be in the past" | 3. Error for Address: "Please enter a complete address (minimum 10 characters)" | 4. Errors display in red below each field | 5. When corrected, error messages clear | 6. Submit button becomes enabled when all valid

Title: Navigation links work from all pages
Preconditions: 1. Application is running | 2. User can access /, /form, /kyc, /users pages
Steps: 1. From Landing Page (/), click "Users" link | 2. From /users, click "Home" in navbar | 3. From Landing Page, click "Apply Now" | 4. From /form, click "Users" button | 5. From /kyc, click "Users" button | 6. Verify URL changes and correct page loads each time
Test Data: No specific test data
Expected Result: 1. All navigation links route correctly | 2. URL updates in browser | 3. Page content loads without errors | 4. Active state highlights current page (where implemented) | 5. Browser back/forward buttons work correctly | 6. No 404 errors or broken routes

Title: Users table displays data correctly with multiple entries
Preconditions: 1. Database has 0 users initially | 2. User Management page loaded
Steps: 1. Add first user with short name and address | 2. Add second user with long name and address | 3. Add third user with special characters in name (O'Brien, Mary-Jane) | 4. Verify table displays all users | 5. Check column alignment and data truncation | 6. Verify sorting (newest first)
Test Data: User 1: "Jo Bo", "1990-01-01", "12 Main St" | User 2: "Christopher Alexander Montgomery", "1985-03-15", "789 Westminster Boulevard, Apartment 15B, San Francisco, CA 94102" | User 3: "Mary-Jane O'Brien", "1992-11-30", "456 Oak Avenue, Boston, MA"
Expected Result: 1. Table shows all 3 users | 2. All columns (Name, DOB, Address, Created At) display correctly | 3. Long text doesn't break layout | 4. Special characters (apostrophe, hyphen) render correctly | 5. Users appear in reverse chronological order (newest at top) | 6. Row alternating colors enhance readability | 7. Table is horizontally scrollable if needed

Title: Attempt to submit form with empty fields
Preconditions: 1. User Management page loaded | 2. All form fields are empty
Steps: 1. Click "Add User" button without filling any fields | 2. Observe validation errors | 3. Verify form is not submitted | 4. Check that no API call is made | 5. Verify error message displays
Test Data: No data entered (all fields empty)
Expected Result: 1. Form does not submit | 2. Error message displays: First validation error ("Name is required") | 3. Field-specific errors show below each empty field | 4. No success message appears | 5. No new entry in users table | 6. Network tab shows no POST request to /api/users | 7. Form remains in editable state

Title: Submit form with name containing invalid characters
Preconditions: 1. User Management page loaded | 2. Form is empty
Steps: 1. Enter name with numbers: "John123" | 2. Enter valid DOB and Address | 3. Click "Add User" button | 4. Observe validation error | 5. Try name with special characters: "John@Smith" | 6. Observe validation error
Test Data: Invalid names: "John123", "User#456", "Test@User", "Name<>123" | Valid: DOB="1990-05-15", Address="123 Main St, City, State"
Expected Result: 1. Validation error displays: "Name can only contain letters, spaces, hyphens, and apostrophes" | 2. Form is not submitted | 3. Error appears in red below Name field | 4. Other fields remain valid | 5. Form can be corrected and resubmitted | 6. Names with O'Brien and Mary-Jane ARE accepted (valid)

Title: Attempt to register user under 18 years old
Preconditions: 1. User Management page loaded | 2. Form is empty
Steps: 1. Calculate a date 17 years ago from today | 2. Enter valid name | 3. Enter DOB that makes user 17 years old | 4. Enter valid address | 5. Click "Add User" button | 6. Observe validation error
Test Data: Name: "Teen User" | DOB: "<17 years ago from today>" | Address: "123 Youth Street, City, State 12345"
Expected Result: 1. Validation error displays: "You must be at least 18 years old" | 2. Form does not submit | 3. Error appears below Date of Birth field | 4. No user is added to database or table | 5. User can correct DOB to valid age and successfully submit

Title: Submit form with incomplete address (less than 10 characters)
Preconditions: 1. User Management page loaded | 2. Form is empty
Steps: 1. Enter valid name | 2. Enter valid DOB (18+ years) | 3. Enter address with only 9 characters: "Main St 1" | 4. Click "Add User" button | 5. Observe validation error | 6. Try with 5 characters | 7. Try with exactly 10 characters
Test Data: Short addresses: "Main St 1" (9 chars), "12345" (5 chars) | Valid: "1234567890" (10 chars)
Expected Result: 1. Error displays: "Please enter a complete address (minimum 10 characters)" | 2. Form does not submit with 9 or fewer characters | 3. Error appears below Address field | 4. With exactly 10 characters, validation passes | 5. Form can be submitted after correction | 6. Character count is accurate

Title: Verify server error handling when database is unavailable
Preconditions: 1. User Management page loaded | 2. Stop database or server | 3. Form is filled with valid data
Steps: 1. Fill form with valid data | 2. Stop database connection or server | 3. Click "Add User" button | 4. Wait for response | 5. Observe error handling | 6. Restart server | 7. Retry submission
Test Data: Name: "Test User" | DOB: "1990-01-01" | Address: "123 Test Street, City, State"
Expected Result: 1. Error message displays after timeout | 2. Message indicates server/connection error | 3. Form data is preserved (not cleared) | 4. User can retry after fixing server | 5. No partial data corruption | 6. Application doesn't crash | 7. After server restart, submission succeeds

Title: Attempt to submit form while a submission is in progress
Preconditions: 1. User Management page loaded | 2. Network is throttled to slow | 3. Form is filled
Steps: 1. Fill form with valid data | 2. Click "Add User" button | 3. Immediately click "Add User" again before first submission completes | 4. Observe behavior
Test Data: Name: "Test User" | DOB: "1990-05-15" | Address: "123 Duplicate Test Street"
Expected Result: 1. Submit button becomes disabled after first click | 2. Button text changes to "Adding User..." | 3. Second click has no effect | 4. Only one API request is sent | 5. Only one user is added to database | 6. After completion, button re-enables | 7. No duplicate entries in table

Title: Long address text handling and display
Preconditions: 1. User Management page loaded | 2. Form is empty
Steps: 1. Enter valid name | 2. Enter valid DOB | 3. Enter very long address (200+ characters) | 4. Submit form | 5. Observe table display | 6. Check if address is fully visible or truncated
Test Data: Name: "Test User" | DOB: "1990-01-01" | Address: "<200+ character address with full details including apartment number, building name, street, landmark, city, state, country, postal code, and additional location details>"
Expected Result: 1. Form accepts long address (no max length restriction observed) | 2. Submission succeeds | 3. Table displays address text | 4. If truncated, full text available on hover or cell expands | 5. Table layout doesn't break | 6. Horizontal scroll appears if needed | 7. Full address is stored in database

Title: Brand compliance verification for Oktawave guidelines
Preconditions: 1. User Management page loaded | 2. Visual inspection tools ready
Steps: 1. Inspect page background gradient | 2. Check primary color usage (#2DB5DA) | 3. Verify secondary color (#939598) | 4. Check font family (Proxima Nova, Montserrat fallback) | 5. Verify navigation active state color | 6. Check button hover states | 7. Verify form input styling | 8. Check table header color
Test Data: Visual inspection data
Expected Result: 1. Background uses dark gradient (#0a0a0f to #12121a) | 2. Primary color #2DB5DA used for: section titles, active nav links, table headers, primary buttons | 3. Secondary color #939598 used appropriately | 4. Font is Proxima Nova or Montserrat fallback | 5. Active navigation link highlighted in brand color | 6. Buttons have brand-colored gradients | 7. Form inputs have proper contrast and borders | 8. Consistent spacing and typography throughout

Title: Page loading state displays correctly
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to /users for first time | 2. Database has users | 3. Network is throttled to slow
Steps: 1. Navigate to /users page | 2. Observe initial loading state | 3. Wait for data to load | 4. Verify transition from loading to data display | 5. Refresh page and repeat
Test Data: Database contains 3-5 users
Expected Result: 1. Loading spinner appears immediately | 2. Text displays "Loading users..." | 3. Loading state styled consistently with brand | 4. After data loads, loading state disappears | 5. Table smoothly transitions to display data | 6. No flash of empty state before loading | 7. Loading doesn't block form interaction

Title: Form reset after successful submission
Preconditions: 1. User Management page loaded | 2. Form is empty
Steps: 1. Fill all form fields with valid data | 2. Submit form | 3. Wait for success message | 4. Verify form fields are cleared | 5. Verify validation errors are cleared | 6. Fill and submit again to confirm form is reusable
Test Data: Submission 1: "User One", "1990-01-01", "123 First St" | Submission 2: "User Two", "1995-02-02", "456 Second Ave"
Expected Result: 1. After success, all form fields return to empty state | 2. No validation errors persist | 3. Form is immediately ready for next entry | 4. Success message clears when starting new entry | 5. Both users appear in table | 6. Form remains functional for multiple submissions

Title: Verify future date is rejected for Date of Birth
Preconditions: 1. User Management page loaded | 2. Form is empty
Steps: 1. Enter valid name | 2. Select tomorrow's date for DOB | 3. Enter valid address | 4. Attempt to submit | 5. Try date 1 year in future | 6. Try today's date | 7. Try yesterday's date (valid)
Test Data: Future dates: tomorrow, 1 year ahead | Today's date | Yesterday's date
Expected Result: 1. Future dates show error: "Date of birth must be in the past" | 2. Today's date may show error depending on validation logic | 3. Yesterday's date passes validation (but may fail 18+ check) | 4. Form does not submit with future dates | 5. Validation is immediate upon field blur | 6. Error clears when valid past date entered

Title: Verify database persistence and audit trail
Preconditions: 1. Database is accessible | 2. User Management page loaded | 3. Admin access to database
Steps: 1. Note current timestamp | 2. Add user via form | 3. Query database directly for user_management table | 4. Verify user record exists | 5. Check created_at and updated_at timestamps | 6. Verify data integrity | 7. Update user (if update functionality exists) | 8. Check updated_at changes
Test Data: Name: "Database Test User" | DOB: "1990-12-31" | Address: "999 Persistence Lane, DB City, State 00000"
Expected Result: 1. User record exists in user_management table with correct UUID | 2. All fields (name, date_of_birth, address) match submitted data | 3. created_at timestamp is accurate (within seconds of submission) | 4. updated_at initially equals created_at | 5. Timestamps are in UTC or consistent timezone | 6. If updated, updated_at changes but created_at remains unchanged | 7. No data corruption or encoding issues
